_comment: Wallet V1 Golden Vectors - Real secp256k1 ECDSA Format (Not HMAC placeholder)

canonical_format_rules.timestamp_critical: Must be UNIX seconds (< 1e10), NOT milliseconds (1e10+)
canonical_format_rules.public_key_format: secp256k1 uncompressed (0x04 + 32 bytes X + 32 bytes Y = 65 bytes = 130 hex chars)

test_vectors.name: Golden Vector 1: Basic THR transfer with real key derivation
test_vectors.mnemonic: abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon abandon about
test_vectors.public_key_format: secp256k1 uncompressed (65 bytes, 0x04 + 32 bytes X + 32 bytes Y)
test_vectors.address_derivation: Custom Thronos hash (or Keccak256 like Ethereum)
test_vectors.backend_verification: ✅ PASS (once ECDSA secp256k1 signing is implemented on all clients)
notes: Private key should be derived from mnemonic using BIP39/BIP32
Public key must be uncompressed secp256k1 format (65 bytes)
Address must be derived from public key using same method as backend
Canonical string must match this format exactly (sorted keys, compact JSON)
Signature must be DER-encoded ECDSA signature using SHA256 hash
All clients must generate identical canonical string for same payload
Backend must accept signature generated by any client

client_signing: 2. Derive private key using BIP39/BIP32 (path: m/44'/60'/0'/0/0)
3. Get uncompressed secp256k1 public key from private key
4. Derive address from public key (same method as backend)
5. Create transaction payload with: from, to, amount, token, nonce, timestamp
6. Serialize to canonical JSON: JSON.stringify(sorted_obj, separators=compact)
8. Sign hash with ECDSA/secp256k1 using private key
backend_verification: 2. Verify no forbidden fields (secret, mnemonic, seed, etc)
6. Verify ECDSA signature using public key
